summary.intent: Address two P1 Codex review findings on PR #687 for the RangeSlider component: blur events cancelling unrelated active drags, and controlled swap-mode drag calculations using stale values.
summary.outcome: Both bugs were fixed with targeted regressions added first (TDD), all existing tests pass, line coverage held at 95.90%, and the fix was committed and pushed to PR #687.

repo: RangeSlider uses a state machine with Dragging/Idle targets; blur transitions must conditionally preserve Dragging state when the blur belongs to an unrelated element during an active drag.
In controlled mode, Context::value.get() returns the controlled prop and does not reflect in-flight drag state — drag_value is the correct observable for pending drag range during controlled drags.
The waiting-for-codex-review skill requires polling until a terminal state (👍 or unresolved threads surfaced), not stopping while 👀 is still present.

code.finding: Blur handler — needed to check whether blur belongs to an unrelated active drag before targeting Idle; unrelated blur must preserve Dragging state.
Controlled swap-mode PointerMove handler — was using Context::value.get() (the controlled prop) instead of drag_value as the base for delta calculations, causing stale math.
Controlled drag regression test: Context::value.get() intentionally stays as the controlled prop during drag; assertions must target drag_value / the commit callback path, not value.get().

workflow: TDD approach used: wrote failing regressions first, verified they fail on current code, then patched — reduced risk of false-positive tests.
cargo +nightly fmt --all must be run before tests to avoid fmt check failures blocking CI.
cargo llvm-cov used to confirm coverage didn't regress after adding new tests and patches.
gh api graphql used to fetch unresolved PR review threads filtered by Codex author login for targeted issue triage.

friction: Assistant stopped polling the review skill too early while Codex still had 👀, requiring user correction before work resumed.
Initial blur fix introduced a regression: ordinary blur still needed to target Idle, requiring a second patch to conditionally branch on whether the blur was from an unrelated drag.
Controlled-drag regression test had to be rewritten because the initial assertion was against Context::value.get(), which is intentionally the controlled prop and not updated during drag.
open_items: Codex threads on PR #687 need to be explicitly replied to and resolved after the push — the transcript ends at commit, before thread replies.
Re-triggering Codex review (@codex review comment) after resolving threads was planned but not shown as completed in the transcript.
